findings.title: CVE-2024-0001 in nginx:1.24 – remote code execution
findings.description: A critical vulnerability was found in nginx 1.24 that allows remote code execution via a specially crafted HTTP/2 request. This affects all deployments running nginx:1.24.
findings.how_to_fix: Update nginx to version 1.25.0 or later to patch this vulnerability.

findings.title: CVE-2024-0002 in nginx:1.24 – denial of service
findings.description: A denial of service vulnerability in nginx 1.24 allows an attacker to crash the worker process via a malformed request.
findings.how_to_fix: Update nginx to version 1.25.0 or later.

findings.title: Container runs in privileged mode
findings.description: The container 'webapp' is configured with privileged: true, granting all capabilities and unrestricted host access.
findings.how_to_fix: Set privileged: false in the compose file to disable privileged mode.

findings.title: Container uses host network mode
findings.description: The container 'webapp' uses network_mode: host, which shares the host network namespace and bypasses Docker network isolation.
findings.how_to_fix: Remove network_mode: host to use the default bridge network, or assign to an overlay network.

findings.title: SSH password authentication is enabled
findings.description: SSH password authentication is currently enabled, making the system vulnerable to brute-force attacks. Key-based authentication is recommended instead.
findings.how_to_fix: Set PasswordAuthentication no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config and restart the SSH service.

findings.title: No firewall is enabled
findings.description: The system does not have an active firewall. This exposes all network services to potential unauthorized access.
findings.how_to_fix: Enable a firewall using ufw or iptables to restrict incoming connections.

findings.title: Root SSH login is permitted
findings.description: SSH permits direct root login, which is a security risk. Root should only log in via su or sudo from a regular user account.

findings.title: Custom security policy violation detected
findings.description: A custom security policy check has failed. This finding requires manual review and remediation by a security administrator.
findings.how_to_fix: Review the custom security policy and apply the necessary configuration changes manually.

findings.title: CVE-2024-0003 in redis:7 – information disclosure
findings.description: A vulnerability in redis:7 allows an authenticated user to read arbitrary memory locations.
findings.how_to_fix: Update redis to version 7.2.5 or later.

findings.title: /etc/shadow has insecure permissions
findings.description: The /etc/shadow file has permissions that are too permissive, potentially allowing unauthorized users to read password hashes.

findings.title: Container exposes ports on all interfaces
findings.description: The container 'database' exposes ports on 0.0.0.0, making it accessible from external networks.
findings.how_to_fix: Bind to 127.0.0.1 only to restrict access to localhost.

findings.title: IP forwarding is enabled
findings.description: IPv4 forwarding is enabled on the system. This should be disabled unless the system is acting as a router.

findings.title: Compose service runs in privileged mode
findings.description: The compose service 'webapp' is configured with privileged: true, granting all capabilities and unrestricted host access.
findings.how_to_fix: Set privileged: false in the compose file to disable privileged mode.

findings.title: Compose file references env_file with permissive permissions
findings.description: The compose file '/home/test/docker-compose.yml' references an env_file whose permissions may expose secret values.
findings.how_to_fix: Restrict the .env file permissions to 600 and ensure it is gitignored.
